articleTitle: Prospective biomarkers of posttraumatic stress disorder in children and adolescents: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
takeaways: Elevated heart rate following trauma exposure showed the most consistent evidence as a prospective biomarker for PTSD persistence in children and adolescents across multiple studies
The research identified only 21 eligible studies from 2039 initially reviewed articles, indicating a significant gap in high-quality research on PTSD biomarkers in young people

Studies examined biomarkers across four biological domains - hormonal, immunological, cardiovascular, and multisystem - but only cardiovascular markers (specifically heart rate) showed reliable predictive associations
The evidence base showed substantial methodological limitations including significant heterogeneity in trauma types, participant selection criteria, and biomarker measurement protocols across studies
Research from low-and-middle income countries was notably absent from the evidence base, despite these regions experiencing endemic chronic and severe trauma exposure
